old_claim.note: Photolithography is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Photolithography and related microfabrication support some PV device prototyping and manufacturing, but the first practical silicon solar cell does not make photolithography a universal hard prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Review article on silicon solar-cell metallization, including early photovoltaic fabrication through photolithographic masks and the later industrial move toward screen printing.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports photolithography as an important historical and specialized fabrication route, but not as a universal prerequisite for all photovoltaic cells.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This demotes a hard requirement to scaling/manufacturing context while preserving semiconductors as the required dependency for photovoltaic cells.

ontology_before: The old required edge made every solar photovoltaic technology depend on photolithography.
ontology_after: Photolithography remains as manufacturing and prototyping infrastructure, while semiconductors preserve the required component dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant added in 2026-06-07-energy-edge-source-scope.json to lock the corrected Energy Systems edge semantics.

demotion_preserves.reason: Photovoltaic cells depend on semiconducting device behavior; this stronger dependency remains required after demoting photolithography.

would_reject_if: A more specific primary or textbook source showed the original dependency type was globally required for the scoped technology.
The dependent node were narrowed to a later industrial product where the old prerequisite is truly mandatory.
A future source-check splits the node and moves this edge onto a more precise downstream technology.
short_reason: Photolithography supports some PV manufacturing but is too narrow as a universal hard prerequisite.
